title: E2E smoke tests — full user journey with Playwright
description: Write Playwright end-to-end tests covering the critical user flows: sign up, log in, log a weight entry, edit it, delete it, and log out.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: user can sign up with a new email and is redirected to dashboard
Test: user can log in with existing credentials
Test: user can log a weight entry and see it in the table
Test: user can edit a weight entry and see the updated value
Test: user can delete a weight entry and it disappears from the table
Test: user can log out and is redirected to /login
All tests pass when run against the running app

steps.details: In src/playwright.config.ts, set baseURL to 'http://localhost:3001'. Set testDir to './tests/e2e'. Configure a single chromium project. Set timeout to 30000ms.

Create src/tests/e2e/auth.spec.ts. Test 1 - signup: navigate to /signup, fill name/email/password, click submit, assert URL is /dashboard. Test 2 - login: navigate to /login, fill email/password, submit, assert URL is /dashboard. Test 3 - logout: log in, click username in top bar, click Logout, assert URL is /login.

Create src/tests/e2e/weight.spec.ts. Use a beforeEach to log in. Test 1 - log entry: click +, fill weight input with '185.5', submit, assert table contains '185.5'. Test 2 - edit entry: click ... on the row, click Edit, change value to '186.0', save, assert table shows '186.0'. Test 3 - delete entry: click ... on the row, click Delete, assert the row is no longer in the table.

steps.description: Run tests and verify all pass
steps.details: Run npx playwright test from the src/ directory with the app running. Fix any failing tests. Take a screenshot of passing test results.

technicalNotes: Use unique emails for each test run to avoid conflicts — e.g. Date.now() in the email
Use page.waitForURL() after form submissions to assert redirects
The playwright.config.ts in src/ already exists — update it rather than creating a new one
